actions-list.actionItem: Motion to reconsider laid on the table Agreed to without objection.
On passage Passed by the Yeas and Nays: (Roll no. 154).
On motion to recommit Failed by the Yeas and Nays: (Roll no. 153).
The previous question on the motion to recommit was ordered pursuant to clause 2(b) of rule XIX.
Ms. Craig moved to recommit to the Committee on Agriculture.
The House adopted the amendments en gros as agreed to by the Committee of the Whole House on the state of the Union.
The previous question was ordered pursuant to the rule.
The House rose from the Committee of the Whole House on the state of the Union to report H.R. 7567.
On agreeing to the Spartz amendment; Agreed to by recorded vote: (Roll no. 152).
On agreeing to the Self amendment; Failed by recorded vote: (Roll no. 151).
On agreeing to the Scholten amendment; as modified Agreed to by recorded vote: (Roll no. 150).
On agreeing to the Moore (WV) amendment; Failed by recorded vote: (Roll no. 149).
On agreeing to the Luna amendment; Agreed to by recorded vote: (Roll no. 148).
On agreeing to the Grothman amendment; Failed by recorded vote: (Roll no. 147).
On agreeing to the Grothman amendment; Agreed to by recorded vote: (Roll no. 146).
On agreeing to the Crawford amendment; Agreed to by recorded vote: (Roll no. 145).
On agreeing to the Bentz amendment; Failed by recorded vote: (Roll no. 144).
On agreeing to the Steube amendment; Agreed to by voice vote.
Pursuant to the provisions of H. Res. 1224, the Committee of the Whole proceeded with 10 minutes of debate on the Steube amendment No. 51.
An amendment, offered by Mr. Steube,  numbered 51 printed in Part B of House Report 119-628 to establish testing standards for honey products
On agreeing to the Steube amendment; Agreed to by voice vote.
Pursuant to the provisions of H. Res. 1224, the Committee of the Whole proceeded with 10 minutes of debate on the Steube amendment No. 50.
An amendment, offered by Mr. Steube,  numbered 50 printed in Part B of House Report 119-628 to prohibit the purchase of agricultural land by foreign adversaries and state sponsors of terrorism

At the conclusion of debate on the Spartz amendment No. 49, the Chair put the question on agreeing to the amendment and by voice vote, announced the ayes had prevailed. Ms. Craig demanded a recorded vote, and the Chair postponed further proceedings until a time to be announced.
Pursuant to the provisions of H. Res. 1224, the Committee of the Whole proceeded with 10 minutes of debate on the Spartz amendment No. 49.

An amendment, offered by Mrs. Spartz,  numbered 49 printed in Part B of House Report 119-628 to remove costly, overreaching emissions mandates on farm equipment that drives up expenses for farmers and consumers.
The House resolved into Committee of the Whole House on the state of the Union for further consideration.

Considered as unfinished business. H.R. 7567 — "To provide for the reform and continuation of agricultural and other programs of the Department of Agriculture through fiscal year 2031, and for other purposes."

actions-list.header: Commission on the National Defense Strategy
actions-list.actionItem: Pursuant to section 1095(b)(1)(A) of the National Defense Authorization Act for fiscal year 2026 (Public Law 119-60), and the order of the House of January 3, 2025, the Speaker appointed Mr. Robert O'Brien, Heber City, Utah.

actions-list.header: Advisory Board for the House of Representatives Child Care Center
actions-list.actionItem: Pursuant to 2 U.S.C. 2062, and the order of the House of January 3, 2025, the Speaker appointed Mr. John Byers, Alexandria, VA; Ms. Rachel Collins, Washington, DC; Ms. Giulia Leganski, Potomac, MD; Mr. Derek Harley, Avon Lake, Ohio; Ms. Lauren Hodge, Alexandria, VA; Mr. Thomas McAllister, Washington, DC; Ms. Anna McCormack, Alexandria, VA; and Mr. Kevin O'Keefe, Falls Church, VA.
The House proceeded with one minute speeches which by direction of the Chair, would be limited to 5 per side of the aisle.
The Chair designated Mr. Morelle to lead the Members in reciting the Pledge of Allegiance to the Flag.
The Speaker announced approval of the Journal.  Pursuant to clause 1, rule I, the Journal stands approved.
Today's prayer was offered by Rabbi Peter Stein, Temple B'rith Kodesh, Rochester, New York
The House convened, starting a new legislative day.
